Captain Elias Thorn stood on the deck of the Heron and waited for the fog to lift. Mira Vale walked to the rail and looked at the gray water. The lighthouse of Port Saffron blinked in the distance. "There," Mira Vale said. Elias Thorn nodded. The Salt Guild controlled every pier along that shore, and its fees had doubled since spring. "They will bleed us dry," Elias Thorn said. Mira Vale smiled and rolled up her charts without answering.
